title: Production of fully functional mature beta cells from human pancreatic progenitors
abstract: Methods are provided for the simple, fast, effective and safe directed differentiation of embryonic stem cells into the mature beta cells of enriched beta clusters, wherein the beta cells rapidly and reliably secrete insulin in response to glucose levels. The cells are useful transplant therapeutics for diabetic individuals. These cells can also be used for drug screening purposes to identify factors/chemicals capable of increasing beta cell functions, proliferation, survival, and resistance to immune assault.
claims: Claims CLAIMS  WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A method of producing endocrine progenitor cells from pluripotent stem cells comprising: (a) incubating pluripotent stem cells (PSCs) in culture medium comprising Wnt3a, Activin A, TG i, and keratinocyte growth factor ( GF) for 3-7 days; (b) exposing the cells resulting from step (a) to medium comprising retinoic acid (RA) for 2-3 days; and (c) culturing the cells resulting from step (b) in medium comprising RA, epidermal growth factor (EGF), and KGF, thereby producing a culture comprising greater than 70% PDX1 + NKX6.1 + endocrine progenitor cells. 2. The method of claim 1 wherein the pluripotent stem cells are human. 3. The method of claim 1 wherein incubating step (a) is five days. 4. The method of claim 1 wherein exposing step (b) is two days. 5. The method of claim 1 wherein culturing step (c) is 3-5 days. 6. The method of claim 1 further comprising incubating the culture comprising PDX1 + NKX6.1 + endocrine progenitor cells in medium comprising Alki, T3, XXi, LDN, NEAA, N-acetyl Cysteine, zinc sulfate, glutamax, heparin, and vitamin C for at least 8 days, thereby producing INS + NKX6. GCG " immature Î²-like cells. 7. The method of claim 6 wherein the culture is incubated for 8- 10 days. 8. The method of claim 6 further comprising sorting the cells in the culture to enrich for INS + NKX6.1 + GCG " immature Î²-like cells. 9. The method of claim 8 wherein the sorting comprises subjecting the cells to flow cytometry. 10. The method of claim 8 or claim 9 further comprising re-aggregating the immature beta-like cells into clusters of about 100-150 Î¼Î·Î¹. 11. The method of claim 10 wherein the re-aggregating is accomplished using Aggrewells, the GravityPLUS Hanging Drop System or Perfecta3D Hanging Drop Plates. 12. The method of claim 8 or claim 9 further comprising culturing the re-aggregated clusters in medium comprising Alki, T3 and low glucose for at least 6 days, thereby producing mature functional beta cells in eBCs. 13. The method of claim 12 wherein the re-aggregated clusters are maintained in culture for 6-14 days. 14. The method of claim 12 wherein the eBCs respond to in vivo glucose challenges within about three days of transplantation of the cells into a subject. 15. The method of claim 12 wherein culturing of the re-aggregated clusters produces no detectable cell types of exocrine pancreatic lineages. 16. The method of claim 15 wherein the cell type of exocrine pancreatic lineage is a duct cell or an acinar cell. 17. The method of claim 12 wherein culturing of the re-aggregated clusters produces less than 4% NKX6.1* PDX1 + C-peptide " pancreatic epithelial progenitor cells in eBCs. 18. The method of claim 12 wherein at least 95% of the eBC-cells express chromogranin A. 19. The method of claim 12 wherein at least 95% of the cells of eBCs stain positive for synaptophysin. 20. The method of claim 19 wherein all of the cells of eBCs stain positive for synaptophysin. 21. The method of claim 12 wherein up to 80% of the eBC-cells are monohormonal C- peptide* cells. 22. The method of claim 21 wherein up to 90% of the eBC-cells are monohormonal C-peptide + cells. 23. The method of claim 12 wherein 80% of the eBC-cells are double positive for C- peptide and N X6.1. 24. The method of claim 23 wherein 87% of the eBC-cells are double positive for C- peptide and NKX6.1. 25. The method of claim 12 wherein the basal oxygen consumption rate of eBCs is more than about 10.799 picomoles of oxygen consumed per minute per nanogram of C-peptide. 26. The method of claim 25 wherein the basal oxygen consumption rate of eBCs is more than about 3.771 picomoles of oxygen consumed per minute per nanogram of C-peptide. 27. The method of claim 12 wherein the extracellular acidification rate of eBCs is at most 0.6922 mpH/minute per nanogram of C-peptide. 28. The method of claim 27 wherein the extracellular acidification rate of eBCs is at most 2.0378 mpH minute per nanogram of C-peptide. 29. A method for producing beta cells from INS + NKX6.1* GCG " immature Î²-like cells comprising sorting the INS + NKX6.1 + GCG " immature Î²-like cells in a culture to enrich for INS + NKX6.1 + GCG " immature Î²-like cells and re-aggregating the cells into islet-sized clusters by maintaining the self-organized clusters in CMRL (5.5mM glucose) containing B27(or FBS), Glutamax, NEAA, Rock inhibitor, ALKi Î , vitamin C, T3, //-acetyl cysteine, zinc sulfate and heparin for at least 6 days, thereby producing mature beta cells. 30. The method of claim 29 wherein the islet-sized clusters are 100-150 Î¼ÏÎ¹ in diameter. 31. The method of claim 29 wherein the islet-sized clusters each comprise about 500- 2,000 cells. 32. The method of claim 29 wherein re-aggregating the immature Î²-like cells into islet- sized clusters is performed in micro-patterned wells. 33. The method of claim 29 wherein re-aggregating the immature Î²-like cells into islet- sized clusters is performed in Aggrewellâ¢ microwells, the GravityPLUS Hanging Drop System or Perfecta3D Hanging Drop Plates. 34. A method of producing mature beta cells from sorted INS + NKX6.1 + GCG " immature Î²-like cells comprising re-aggregating the cells by maintaining the cells in CMRL (5.5mM glucose) containing B27(or FBS), Glutamax, NEAA, Rock inhibitor, ALKi Î , vitamin C, T3, N-acetyl cysteine, zinc sulfate and heparin for at least 6 days, thereby producing mature beta cells. 36. The method of claim 35 wherein the cells are maintained for 6-14 days. 37. The beta cell produced according to the method of claim 12, 14, or 20. 38. A method of treating diabetes mellitus comprising administering an effective amount of the beta cell of claim 37. 39. The method of claim 38 wherein the diabetes mellitus is type I or type II diabetes mellitus. 40. A method of generating a PDX1 + progenitor cell comprising contacting an embryonic stem cell with an effective amount of a retinoic acid compound, thereby inducing formation of a PDX1* progenitor cell. 41. The method according to claim 40 wherein the embryonic stem cell is a human embryonic stem cell. 42. The method according to claim 40 wherein the embryonic stem cell is contacted with a retinoic acid compound in vitro. 43. The method according to claim 40 further comprising not contacting the embryonic stem cell with a bone morphogenic protein (BMP) inhibitor prior to expression of NKX6,l by the ceIl. 44. The method according to claim 36 further comprising contacting the cell with effective amounts of epidermal growth factor and keratinocyte growth factor, thereby inducing formation of a PDX 17NKX6.1 * progenitor cell. 45. The method according to claim 44 wherein the cell expresses NKX6.1 prior to the cell contacting at least one of epidermal growth factor and keratinocyte growth factor. 46. The method according to claim 44 wherein the cell expresses NKX6.1 prior to contacting epidermal growth factor and keratinocyte growth factor. 47. The method according to claim 44 further comprising inducing the PDX17NKX6.1* progenitor cell to express NEUROG3, resulting in production of an INS + /NKX6.1 + beta-like cell. 48. The method according to claim 47 wherein the NEUROG3 expression is induced by contacting the PDX1 + /NKX6.1 + progenitor cell with an effective amount of an inliibitor of bone morphogenetic protein, an inhibitor of TGF /ALK, or an inhibitor of sonic hedgehog. 49. The method according to claim 48 wherein the PDX1 + NKX6.1 + progenitor cell is contacted by an effective amount of bone morphogenetic protein and an effective amount of a inhibitor of TGF /ALK. 50. The method according to claim 49 wherein the PDX1 + NKX6.1 + progenitor cell is contacted by an effective amount of an inhibitor of bone morphogenetic protein and an effective amount of an inhibitor of sonic hedgehog. 51. The method according to claim 48 wherein the inhibitor of bone morphogenetic protein is Noggin or the inhibitor of sonic hedgehog is Cyclopamine. 52. The method according to claim 47 wherein the NEUROG3 expression is induced by exposure of the PDX1 + NKX6.1 + progenitor cell to effective amounts of a TATA-Binding Protein, an Activin receptor-Like Kinase inhibitor, Noggin and Keratinocyte Growth Factor. 53. The method according to claim 47 wherein the NEUROG3 expression begins before expression of NKX2.2 is detected. 54. The method according to claim 40 wherein no more than 5% of the generated cells are polyhormonal cells. 55. The method according to claim 47 wherein the INS + NKX6.1 + beta-like cell is responsive to glucose levels. 56. The method according to claim 55 wherein the INS"YNKX6.1 + beta-like cell secretes an increased level of insulin in response to an increased glucose level. 57. The method according to claim 47 wherein the INS + /NKX6.1 + beta-like cell does not express a detectable level of the Ki67 marker. 58. The method according to claim 47 further compromising transplanting the INS + /NKX6.1 + beta-like cell into a human. 59. The method according to claim 58 wherein the human is diabetic. 60. The method according to claim 55 wherein the INS + /NKX6.1 + beta-like cell is an insulin + beta cell, a glucagon " beta cell, or an insulinVglucagon ' beta cell. 61. The method according to claim 60 further comprising (a) sorting the INS-expressing cells to obtain immature beta cells; and (b) re-aggregating the immature beta cells into islet-sized clusters by culturing the cells in micro-patterned wells comprising CMRL (5.5mM glucose) containing B27(or FBS), Glutamax, NEAA, Rock inhibitor, ALKi II, vitamin C, T3, N-acetyl cysteine, zinc sulfate and heparin to produce beta cell islet-like clusters. 62. The method according to claim 61 wherein greater than 95% of the cells are beta cells.
